parts.text:
In /Users/jaronswab/go/src/github.com/jrswab/axe, run this Go test to check if `` text from `<head>` leaks into stripHTML output: ```bash cd /Users/jaronswab/go/src/github.com/jrswab/axe && go test ./internal/tool/ -run TestStripHTML_BasicExtraction -v -count=1 ``` Also, write and run a quick ad-hoc test. Create a temporary test file or just use `go test -run` to check: what does `stripHTML("<html><head><title>Page Title

Body text

")` return? You can do this by temporarily adding a test: ```go func TestStripHTML_HeadContentCheck(t *testing.T) { got := stripHTML("Page Title

Body text

") t.Logf("stripHTML output: %q", got) if strings.Contains(got, "Page Title") { t.Errorf("stripHTML leaked content: %q", got) } } ``` Add this test to url_fetch_test.go, run it, and report the result. Then REMOVE the test afterward (it was just for investigation). Return what the output was — does `<title>` content leak into the stripped text?
I'll run the existing test and add a temporary investigation test in parallel.

parts.text:
**Yes, `<title>` content leaks into `stripHTML` output.**

The raw output was:
```
"Page Title Body text"
```

`stripHTML` does **not** suppress `<head>` content — it strips HTML tags but treats all text nodes equally regardless of which element they're in. The text inside `<title>` ("Page Title") is emitted just like body text, separated by a space.

The existing `TestStripHTML_BasicExtraction` passes because its test cases don't include a `<head>/<title>` scenario, so the bug was untested. The function would need to explicitly skip text nodes that are descendants of `<head>` (or at minimum `<title>`, `<script>`, `<style>`) to fix this.
